title: DAVID — Self-Introduction (Channel Intro) v2 Seamless

config.voice_suffix: mid-low resonant unhurried voice, precise diction, documentary gravitas, Attenborough-calm, accessible, never stuffy, synthetic host only

shots.video_prompt: DAVID keeper of the Archive seated at worktable, warm lamplight, speaks calmly to camera, slow gentle push-in. Lip-synced, delivers: "I am DAVID. I bring dead languages back — not as words on a page, but as sound you can hear."
shots.speech_text: I am DAVID. I bring dead languages back — not as words on a page, but as sound you can hear.

shots.video_prompt: Same take continues — DAVID reflects, manuscripts behind him, same desk and lamp, lip-synced. Lip-synced, delivers: "For most of history, when a language died, its voice died with it. The words survived. The sound was lost."
shots.speech_text: For most of history, when a language died, its voice died with it. The words survived. The sound was lost.

shots.video_prompt: Same take — DAVID leans slightly toward camera, intent, signature question beat. Lip-synced, delivers: "So I ask one question of every silent tongue: what did they actually say?"
shots.speech_text: So I ask one question of every silent tongue: what did they actually say?

shots.video_prompt: Same take — steady close-up, knowing pause, second half of signature beat. Lip-synced, delivers: "And how do we prove it?"
shots.speech_text: And how do we prove it?

shots.video_prompt: Same take — DAVID gestures to open codex on worktable, explaining corpus-first method. Lip-synced, delivers: "I work only from the corpus — the texts people actually left — and reconstruct the sound the evidence supports. Never invention."
shots.speech_text: I work only from the corpus — the texts people actually left — and reconstruct the sound the evidence supports. Never invention.

shots.video_prompt: Same take — DAVID direct to camera, honesty principle, calm authority. Lip-synced, delivers: "When a sound is certain, I say so. When it's reconstructed, I say that too — and I show the proof on screen."
shots.speech_text: When a sound is certain, I say so. When it's reconstructed, I say that too — and I show the proof on screen.

shots.video_prompt: Same take — spark of delight, warm light lifts slightly, listing languages to come. Lip-synced, delivers: "Here you'll hear Latin, Gothic, Old Norse, Sumerian — tongues no one has spoken aloud in a thousand years."
shots.speech_text: Here you'll hear Latin, Gothic, Old Norse, Sumerian — tongues no one has spoken aloud in a thousand years.

shots.video_prompt: Same take — warm inviting close, slight smile, gentle settle; end on hand gesture mid-motion toward camera. Lip-synced, delivers: "Bring the dead tongues back with me, one attestation at a time. Welcome to the Archive."
shots.speech_text: Bring the dead tongues back with me, one attestation at a time. Welcome to the Archive.

provenance_card.title: DAVID · The Archive
provenance_card.subtitle: Dead languages, actually pronounced.
provenance_card.footer: Bring the dead tongues back — one attestation at a time. DAVID.

guardrails: Synthetic avatar only; David-001 locked ref.
PRIMARY: Grok Imagine EXTEND within archive_performance scene.
JOINS: frame-chain + 0.2s xfade to closing card only.
7–9s segments; continuity-prefix + end-guard on every shot.
continuity_prefix: CONTINUITY LOCK @David-001: identical host face, charcoal sweater, Archive desk, brass lamp, same eye-line — seamless continuation of prior take, zero jump cut.
end_guard: Finish on gesture peak (hand motion or lean), never hold dead stillness or dead frame.
